title: Forensic Sciences Congress, in Collaboration with Marmara and Doğuş Universities, Held on February 25 with High Attendance
content:
Student

News

Details

Forensic Sciences Congress, in Collaboration with Marmara and Doğuş Universities, Held on February 25 with High Attendance

The Forensic Sciences Congress, organized in collaboration with Marmara University and Doğuş University and with the active contribution of student clubs, was successfully held on February 25, 2026, featuring high participation and strong academic content.

The congress was brought to life as a joint organization of the Doğuş University  Doctors by Heart Club and the Marmara University Forensic Sciences Club, serving as an exemplary academic activity where students played an active role in scientific events. The dedicated efforts of the student clubs during the organization process strengthened the corporate quality of the event.

The moderation of the program was undertaken by Lecturer Gözde MASATCIOĞLU, an Instructor in the Autopsy Assistantship Program at Doğuş University Vocational School and the advisor of the Doğuştan Sağlıkçılar Club. The academic coordination and scientific flow of the congress were conducted meticulously.

Within the scope of the congress;

* Prof. Dr. Aylin YALÇIN SARIBEY

* Prof. Dr. Emel Hülya YÜKSELOĞLU

* Asst. Prof. Dr. Yavuz TURAN

addressed the multidisciplinary structure of forensic sciences by sharing the latest developments in their respective fields. The presentations made a significant contribution to the students, allowing them to evaluate their theoretical knowledge from a practical perspective and develop their professional visions.

The congress, which was highly productive in terms of participation, academic interaction, and interdisciplinary content, strongly reflected the scientific productivity and student-centered approach of both Marmara University and Doğuş University.

We would like to thank everyone who contributed to this successful organization, especially the moderator and academic coordinator Lecturer Gözde MASATCIOĞLU, as well as all our speakers, student clubs, and contributing students.
